rationale: Both candidates independently surface the real latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py still calling the removed get_cache_scale), which neither ground-truth reviewer caught. X frames it as an unresolved 'major/FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' blocker with a concrete fix, which is more urgently actionable but conflicts with the PR's actual approved/merged outcome and inflates severity elsewhere (tagging a 'keep this test' suggestion as 'major'). Y verifies against actual upstream vLLM source (AutoWeightsLoader, weight_utils, fp8.py line citations), correctly lands on APPROVE matching the real outcome, better calibrates severity (blocker table all PASS, findings as non-blocking/minor), and its test-completeness critique is more technically precise than X's. Neither candidate explicitly recovers the ground truth's specific 'verify on a real FP8 checkpoint' / '#4808 coordination' points, capping recall for both around 0.4-0.45.
